refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry data to verify availability meets or exceeds the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously to detect outages.
FR2: Manage network link recovery to restore connectivity promptly after outages.
FR3: Report link availability status to teleoperation control systems.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths to increase link reliability.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and automatic failover mechanisms.
DP3: Provide link status reporting and alerting to teleoperation control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths to increase link reliability.

potential_sources: Dependency between link monitoring and failover control
Interaction between link recovery and reporting
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the FRs with minimal coupling; redundancy isolates failure modes and supports modular recovery.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
Risk that redundancy alone may not meet availability if failover is slow.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 (real-time monitoring and failover) in next refinement level to address failover speed.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link communication, including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation commands and telemetry.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands dependent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion due to cross-cutting concerns; containment moderate as link management is a core teleoperation function.
Low containment; vehicle module primarily physical hardware, limited direct link management.
High containment and low diffusion; teleoperation module is the natural owner of link availability functions.
Some interface for safety alerts but limited direct role in link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module centralizes control and minimizes diffusion, improving maintainability and clarity.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with NGHE Solution for telemetry reporting.
Risk of insufficient coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with NGHE Solution and Safety Services to mitigate coupling risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network paths) logically satisfies the refined FRs with clear traceability and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Cross-module telemetry reporting may increase diffusion if not well managed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for link availability functions, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link status monitoring is feasible within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution for telemetry and tasking.
Plan next level refinement to incorporate failover control and monitoring.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, incorporating real-time monitoring and failover mechanisms (DP2) in this level will strengthen the solution and address risks related to failover speed and availability targets.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously to detect outages.
FR2: Manage network link recovery to restore connectivity promptly after outages.
FR3: Report link availability status to teleoperation control systems.

revise.why: To include real-time monitoring and automatic failover mechanisms alongside redundancy to improve link availability robustness.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry data is reliable for verification.
open_risks: Complexity of redundancy and failover may impact maintainability.
Coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
evidence_needed: Performance data on failover speed and link recovery times.
Telemetry logs demonstrating link availability over evaluation period.
